skill.name: competitor-alternatives
skill.description: Create SEO-focused competitor alternative and comparison pages using structured research, fair positioning, reusable competitor data, and modular page sections.
skill.summary: Build fair, SEO-focused competitor alternative and comparison pages.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are markdown examples, URL templates, YAML snippets, and ordinary prose rather than executable code or malicious instructions. No prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or command execution behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are Markdown Syntax
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The flagged lines contain URL patterns, fenced markdown examples, YAML examples, JSON-LD examples, and comparison-page templates, not shell commands or executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation inside SKILL.md, including code fences and route placeholders. I found no shell invocation, Ruby execution context, or user-controlled command path.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And Reconnaissance Detections Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are false positives. The flagged lines are frontmatter, guidance text, pricing placeholders, and content templates; they do not define cryptographic algorithms or collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context shows marketing and content-planning prose only. There is no cryptographic operation, hash implementation, host enumeration, environment probing, or system reconnaissance command.

content.value_statement: Competitive search pages often become shallow feature tables that users do not trust. This skill helps create fair, researched alternative and versus pages with reusable data and stronger buyer guidance.
actual_capabilities: Defines four competitor page formats: singular alternative, plural alternatives, direct versus, and competitor-versus-competitor.
Provides section structures for comparison summaries, feature analysis, pricing, support, migration, and social proof.
Recommends centralized competitor data files to keep pricing, features, strengths, and weaknesses consistent.
Guides research across product trials, pricing pages, review sites, customer interviews, and competitor content.
Includes SEO guidance for keywords, internal linking, index pages, and FAQ schema planning.
Lists discovery questions for gathering product differentiators, competitors, quotes, pricing, and migration details.
limitations: It does not fetch current competitor pricing or search volume by itself.
It relies on the user to verify factual claims before publishing.
It does not generate production website code or deploy pages.
It cannot replace legal review for comparative advertising claims.

use_cases.title: Create Switcher Landing Pages
use_cases.description: Plan pages for buyers searching for an alternative to a specific competitor, including pain points, migration notes, and proof.

use_cases.title: Build Sales Comparison Assets
use_cases.description: Turn competitor research into fair comparison sections that sales teams can use during late-stage evaluations.

use_cases.title: Scale SEO Comparison Hubs
use_cases.description: Design index pages, reusable competitor profiles, and page templates for a larger comparison content library.

prompt_templates.title: Draft One Alternative Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an alternative page outline for [Competitor]. Our product is [Product], and our strongest differentiators are [Differentiators]. Include honest tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Products Fairly
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a [Product] vs [Competitor] page structure. Cover features, pricing, support, integrations, ideal users, migration, and calls to action.

prompt_templates.title: Create Competitor Data Profiles
prompt_templates.prompt: Design centralized competitor data profiles for [Competitors]. Include fields for pricing, features, strengths, weaknesses, reviews, ideal users, and migration notes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Comparison Content Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized page set for our competitive SEO program. Use these competitors: [List]. Include page formats, internal links, research tasks, and update cadence.

output_examples.input: A project management startup needs a ClickUp alternative page.
output_examples.output: A structured page plan with search intent, switcher pain points, comparison sections, migration guidance, proof points, and a clear CTA.

output_examples.input: A content team wants a hub for several CRM comparison pages.
output_examples.output: An index page concept with grouped comparison links, methodology notes, freshness signals, filters, and internal linking recommendations.

output_examples.input: A sales team needs a fair HubSpot versus Salesforce asset.
output_examples.output: A balanced comparison framework that explains ideal buyers, support models, pricing considerations, feature depth, and honest tradeoffs.

best_practices: Verify competitor pricing, features, and claims before publishing each page.
Acknowledge where competitors are stronger to build trust with evaluators.
Store competitor facts in reusable profiles so updates stay consistent across pages.
anti_patterns: Do not publish comparison pages based only on feature checkmarks.
Do not hide competitor strengths or exaggerate your own capabilities.
Do not reuse stale pricing, old screenshots, or unsupported customer claims.

faq.question: What types of pages does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It supports alternative pages, alternatives roundup pages, direct versus pages, competitor-versus-competitor pages, and comparison index pages.

faq.question: Does it perform live competitor research?
faq.answer: No. It provides the research framework, but users must gather and verify current product, pricing, and review data.

faq.question: Can it help with programmatic SEO?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends centralized competitor data and repeatable page structures that can support larger comparison libraries.

faq.question: How should teams keep pages accurate?
faq.answer: Review pricing and major features quarterly, update pages when customers mention changes, and do a full annual refresh.

faq.question: Is it only for SaaS companies?
faq.answer: No. The framework is most natural for SaaS, but it can apply to many products with clear competitors and buyer comparisons.

faq.question: Does it generate legal-safe claims automatically?
faq.answer: No. Teams should verify comparative claims and seek legal review when claims may affect advertising compliance.
